checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=67188282.97 (2010-12-31) to AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=79766973.48 (2011-12-31), +18.7%, versus Revenues=216659277.52 (FY2010) to Revenues=287715260.21 (FY2011), +32.8% -- receivables grew slower than revenue in this period, showing inconsistency across years

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill=106741826.70 (2010-12-31) to Goodwill=128170170.72 (2014-12-31), roughly +20% over 4 years while Revenues=216659277.52 (FY2010) to Revenues=407508716.37 (FY2014), roughly +88%
OtherAssetsNoncurrent=907498.05 (2010-12-31) to OtherAssetsNoncurrent=1259107.11 (2014-12-31) - small balances, growth well below revenue growth

Assets=417642238.20 (2015-03-31) jumped to Assets=734132491.00 (2015-06-30) while Goodwill only moved from Goodwill=125403106.42 (2015-03-31) to Goodwill=138339596.29 (2015-06-30) -- the large asset increase is not explained by goodwill/intangible growth, but no intangible asset line item is provided to confirm the source

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: Gross margin computed from Revenues=216659277.52 and CostOfServices=64320935.81 (FY2010) = 70.3%; Revenues=407508716.37 and CostOfServices=120670002.70 (FY2014) = 70.4% - margin stable across the period
No InventoryNet concept is present in the provided data set, consistent with a services company (SalesRevenueServicesNet, CostOfServices tags used)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: Quarterly OperatingIncomeLoss swings widely: OperatingIncomeLoss=1321010.12 (2012-01-01/2012-03-31) vs OperatingIncomeLoss=-7376362.01 (2012-04-01/2012-06-30) vs OperatingIncomeLoss=-1654048.28 (2012-07-01/2012-09-30)

Revenues grew steadily each quarter, e.g., Revenues=95205582.79 (Q1 2014), Revenues=99060901.90 (Q2 2014), Revenues=101689303.47 (Q3 2014), Revenues=111552928.20 (Q4 2014) - smooth compared to volatile NetIncomeLoss over same quarters (-968162.99, -3961792.28, -4037313.95, -3293239.84)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=2573926.93 (2014-12-31) then AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=0.0 (2015-03-31), then AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=2920583.75 (2015-06-30), then again AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=0.0 (2015-09-30), while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent remained large at AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=103380493.56 (2015-03-31) and AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=96358216.73 (2015-09-30)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filed 2011-05-02, 10-K/A filed 2012-04-27, 10-K/A filed 2013-04-29, 10-K/A filed 2014-04-29, 10-K/A filed 2015-04-24 - a 10-K amendment filed in every fiscal year in the record

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Point-in-time series available from Assets=350468811.95 (2010-12-31) through Assets=675545011.47 (2015-09-30), spanning roughly 5 years of quarterly and annual data across balance sheet, income statement and cash flow concepts
No InventoryNet, IntangibleAssetsNet, or detailed stock-based compensation concepts were provided, limiting full assessment of CL2 and CL4 mechanisms

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential understatement of the allowance for doubtful accounts (reserve zeroed to $0 in two separate quarters despite ~$100M gross receivables), which would understate bad debt expense and overstate net receivable carrying value and reported earnings in those periods.

Receivables grew markedly faster than revenue in FY2013 (+31.7% vs +12.4%), suggestive of a slowdown in collections, channel-stuffing, or aggressive revenue recognition ahead of cash receipt, which would inflate revenue/receivables relative to underlying cash-collected business activity in that period.

Persistent multi-year divergence where reported net income/operating income is negative or marginal while operating cash flow is consistently strongly positive and growing, consistent with heavy non-cash charges (D&A, stock-based compensation, deferred revenue build-up) masking underlying profitability trends; while not necessarily a misstatement per se, the scale and persistence of the gap warrant scrutiny of expense recognition and capitalization policies.

top_signals: Allowance for doubtful accounts dropped to $0 in two separate quarters (2015-03-31 and 2015-09-30) despite ~$100M gross receivables, an unusual and potentially reserve-understating pattern
Persistent, large, multi-year divergence between negative/marginal net income and strongly positive, growing operating cash flow (FY2011-FY2014)
Receivables grew far faster than revenue in FY2013 (+31.7% vs +12.4% revenue growth), reversing prior-year pattern of receivables growing slower than revenue
Recurring annual 10-K/A amendments every fiscal year plus a 10-Q/A amendment, suggesting recurring post-filing corrections
Unexplained near-doubling of total assets and stockholders' equity in Q2 2015 not matched by proportional goodwill/intangible growth, coincident with merger-related tender offer/S-4 filings
